secondary_archetype: BizOps / Strategy & Ops — QA / AI Operations focus

level_signals: Described explicitly as 'senior IC / early people-manager role'
Comp range $95,200–$140,000 base consistent with AM / Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 6–8+ years of experience in strategy, program management, operations, product ops, or quality systems—ideally in AI-enabled or data-driven environments
must_haves.qualifier: ideally in AI-enabled or data-driven environments

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You've worked on large-scale process or platform launches, where accuracy, governance, and change management mattered as much as speed
You are comfortable operating at the intersection of AI models, business rules, and operational workflows
You have a strong intuition for signal quality—understanding false positives, calibration tradeoffs, thresholds, and downstream impact
You bring structured thinking and execution rigor, especially in ambiguous problem spaces with multiple stakeholders
You can partner effectively with ML and Engineering teams, even if you are not a model builder yourself
You care deeply about building systems that are accurate, fair, trusted, and scalable

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Hands-on experience with LLMs, prompt design, conversational analytics, or AI QA systems is a strong plus

scope.surface: AI-led Quality Assurance programs across Customer Support, Fraud, Integrity, Trust & Safety, and in-house support domains within Performance Excellence (CXI)
scope.business_model: B2C / B2B marketplace (food and goods delivery)
primary_metrics_inferred: AI signal precision / false positive and false negative rates
QA automation coverage across customer interactions
Rollout readiness and adoption of AQA systems
core_responsibilities: Own AI-led QA system expansion into complex domains (Customer Support, Fraud, Integrity, Risk, In-House), defining what is automatable vs. human-judgment required
Design and operationalize quality signals at scale, including rubric logic, precision thresholds, false-positive controls, and rollout gates
Drive signal accuracy and trust, partnering with ML and Calibration teams to improve precision, reduce FP/FN rates, and close dispute feedback loops
Translate ambiguous quality problems into structured systems, from intake → signal design → calibration → launch → governance
Influence the QA tech and model roadmap, prioritizing investments in automation, tooling, and infrastructure that improve signal reliability
Establish durable process frameworks (intake, SLAs, readiness criteria, documentation) that allow AI quality programs to scale safely and repeatably
Lead cross-functional execution across Product, Engineering, Ops, and Policy without formal authority—driving clarity, alignment, and delivery

green_flags: Clear product surface and scope of ownership (not vague)
Role fills a genuine gap—expanding AQA into high-impact domains after Tier 1 success
Explicit application deadline (5/25/26) signals active, live opening
Comprehensive benefits package including 16 weeks paid parental leave, 401(k) match, wellness benefits
Inclusive hiring language and non-discrimination commitments are explicit and detailed
red_flags: Location type is ambiguous—JD references NYC-specific AEDT/Covey notice but does not clearly state whether the role is fully remote, hybrid, or onsite; left as 'unclear'
People-management scope is described as 'early' and the role is simultaneously framed as senior IC, creating ambiguity about actual direct reports
Pay range ($95,200–$140,000) is noted as 'national' but localized per work location—actual comp could vary significantly by city

scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated explicitly; inferred from responsibilities)
location.type (set to 'unclear' — JD mentions NYC AEDT notice and remote jobs associated with NYC office, but role location is not explicitly stated)

ambiguities: Location type is not clearly defined in the JD body — the Covey/AEDT notice references 'NYC or Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC' but the role itself is not explicitly labeled remote, hybrid, or onsite. Treated as 'unclear' with remote_us_eligible: true.
People management is described as 'early people-manager role' in one place and framed as senior IC in another — actual direct report count and managerial scope are not stated.
The 6–8+ years range in the JD is unusual phrasing; the minimum is set to 6 years and max left null as the '+' implies open-ended.
